title: parastrephia phyliciformis
intro: Parastrephia phyliciformis, also known as chilca or tarwi, is a plant species native to South America that belongs to the daisy family and is often considered a pseudocereal due to its high protein content. Traditionally, the plant's leaves and seeds have been used in herbal medicine to treat various ailments, including fever, inflammation, and respiratory issues, due to its antimicrobial, antifungal, and anti-inflammatory properties. The plant's health benefits are attributed to its high content of protein, fiber, and essential minerals, making it a valuable source of nutrition for people with high altitude adaptations in Andean regions. Parastrephia phyliciformis can be prepared into various herbal remedies, including teas, infusions, and flour made from its seeds, which can be used to treat digestive issues and provide essential nutrients.
